commits.message:
mit-styling-aj1.7.4: the measure — specimen plates pulled along a printer's pica rule (#27)

Rethink kind=horizontal from the baseline glowing rounded cards (translated on
raw p) into a galley-measure SPECIMEN: matte plates ride a continuous calibrated
pica rule, pulled sideways past a FIXED jade registration gate; the catalogue
label honestly names the plate centred under it (real index/total + title). It
wears the gallery's shared print-specimen vocabulary (jade crop-mark sheet frame
+ tracked mono label, no bloom) while its mechanic — a measure, not a film strip
— duplicates no sibling.

Travel is driven by n.w (read-beat applied), not raw p, so the opening plate
holds for the beat before the strip moves — applying bmf's pause-before-animating
feedback via beat:'dwell' (data on the node, not a guard). Updates the read-beat
SCOPE comment, which named horizontal's filmstrip as a raw-p ambient graphic
that no longer exists.

Verified in Chrome across the full scroll range: zero console errors, the
opening plate holds ~26% before travel, plates light/label track in order.
